What's an example of a time like a client came in and you said you or you had to say you couldn't do?
Whatever they were asking.
Where are your limits?
Yeah, I mean, on paid media, I think it's really easy to disqualify people.
It's like if they don't have a high enough ACV where we don't think they're ever going to see a positive ROI, we'll tell them that.
And we'll say like, hey, I don't think it's responsible for us to take you on as a client because you're not going to see a positive ROI.
In order to do the right amount of testing, you're going to have to spend a minimum of 15 to 20K a month.
And then to get there, you're going to have to close this many deals to be ROI positive, given your ACV.
It's just not a good fit.
It's not going to make sense because our retainer on top of the ad spend,
It's just not going to work out for you financially.
And then on the go-to-market engineering side, it's like if they have an audience that doesn't do well in terms of outbound messaging and they want to do outbound with us.
So go-to-market engineering isn't only outbound.
We do a lot of workflows and CRM enrichment these days as well.

But if they wanted to do outbound with us and they're reaching out to cybersecurity professionals or something like that, we're going to tell them, like, hey, very unlikely you're going to see success doing this.
People in cybersecurity are extremely skeptical of any message.
They always think they're being attacked.
So sending outbound messages is not going to be a great fit for you.
That makes sense.
